name: Rasatala
description: Rasatala is one of the seven lower realms or Patalas in Hindu cosmology, situated beneath the earth. It is often described as a beautiful and alluring realm, filled with precious gems, fertile lands, and enchanting forests. Unlike some of the other lower realms which are associated with suffering and torment, Rasatala is primarily characterized by pleasure and abundance. It is inhabited by powerful and sometimes malevolent beings, including Danavas and Daityas, who are often depicted as skilled artisans, warriors, and sorcerers. These beings lead opulent lives, indulging in sensory pleasures and wielding considerable power. Rasatala is considered a realm of great material prosperity, but it is also associated with attachment, illusion, and the potential for spiritual stagnation. The realm's abundance can distract its inhabitants from seeking higher truths and liberation (moksha). Certain narratives describe Rasatala as a place of conflict between the Devas (gods) and the Asuras (demons) for control over its resources and inhabitants. Access to Rasatala is sometimes depicted as being through subterranean passages or through mystical portals. The overall mythological importance of Rasatala lies in its representation of the allure of material pleasures and the challenges they pose on the path to spiritual enlightenment.
subtitle: A realm of pleasure and abundance.

keyMyths.title: Vishnu's Boar Avatar
keyMyths.description: Vishnu, in his Varaha (boar) avatar, descended into the lower realms, possibly including Rasatala, to rescue the earth goddess Bhumi from the clutches of the demon Hiranyaksha.

keyMyths.title: Description of Patala
keyMyths.description: The Puranas describe Patala, including Rasatala, as being richly adorned with jewels and inhabited by powerful beings who enjoy immense wealth and pleasure.

sources.author: Vettam Mani
sources.description: Comprehensive compilation of Hindu mythological narratives.
